sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value1 FLOAT, value2 FLOAT, ip_address INET);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, value1, value2, ip_address) VALUES (1, 1.2, 5.0, inet '192.168.1.5');
INSERT INTO test_data (id, value1, value2, ip_address) VALUES (2, NULL, 3.0, inet '192.168.1.6');
INSERT INTO test_data (id, value1, value2, ip_address) VALUES (3, 1.0, 7.0, inet NULL);
SELECT id, sql_mul(value1, value2) AS product FROM test_data WHERE sql_mul(value1, value2) < 10.0;
SELECT a.id, sql_mul(a.value1, a.value2) AS product FROM test_data a JOIN test_data b ON sql_mul(a.value1, b.value2) < 15.0;
SELECT id, value1, value2 FROM test_data WHERE (value1 IS NOT NULL AND sql_mul(value1, value2) < 10.0) OR (value2 IS NULL);
SELECT id, value1, value2 FROM test_data WHERE inet '192.168.1.5' < inet '192.168.1.6' AND sql_mul(value1, value2) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT id, sql_mul(value1, value2) AS product FROM test_data GROUP BY id HAVING product < 10.0) AS subquery;
SELECT a.id FROM test_data a WHERE a.value1 < (SELECT sql_mul(b.value1, 2) FROM test_data b WHERE b.value2 > 5.0 LIMIT 1);
SELECT DISTINCT sql_mul(value1, value2) FROM test_data WHERE value1 IS NOT NULL UNION SELECT 0.0;
SELECT ip_address, sql_mul(value1, 1.0) AS modified_value FROM test_data WHERE sql_mul(value1, value2) < 10.0;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE temp_data AS SELECT id, sql_mul(value1, value2) AS product FROM test_data;
INSERT INTO temp_data (id, product) SELECT id, sql_mul(value1, value2) FROM test_data WHERE value1 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT AVG(product) FROM temp_data WHERE product IS NOT NULL;
ALTER TABLE test_data ADD COLUMN extra_value FLOAT DEFAULT 0.0;
SELECT id, sql_mul(value1, value2), ip_address FROM test_data WHERE sql_mul(value1, value3) IS NULL;
